invariant_descriptions.APM-0: conduct_interview() is the sole entry point for post-mortem creation. Direct AgentReasoningEntry instantiation outside conduct_interview() is forbidden.
invariant_descriptions.APM-DETERM-0: entry_digest MUST be deterministic: sha256(agent_id:mutation_id:identified_gap)[:16], prefixed sha256:. No RNG, datetime, uuid4, or process-state inputs.
invariant_descriptions.APM-PERSIST-0: _persist() MUST use Path.open(a) in append mode. Direct builtins.open() is forbidden. ledger_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True) MUST precede every write.
invariant_descriptions.APM-GAP-0: identified_gap MUST be a non-empty string. Empty or whitespace-only is a Hard failure before persist.
invariant_descriptions.APM-LOAD-0: agent_recurring_gaps() MUST be fail-open: any corrupt JSONL line silently skipped. Method MUST NOT raise on partial ledger corruption.
invariant_descriptions.APM-CHAIN-0: Every entry MUST carry: agent_id, mutation_id, epoch_id, rejection_reasons (non-empty list), entry_digest. Missing any field is a Hard failure before persist.

notes: Innovation #17 — Agent Post-Mortem Interviews. After governance rejection, agents articulate why they believed a mutation would pass, what constitutional gap they missed, and proposed correction. These interviews become calibration inputs to agent selection pressure. World-first: constitutional-governed post-mortem interviews for autonomous mutation agents.
